question: Will real GDP increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model has revised Q1 2026 real GDP growth down to 1.6% as of early April from over 2% in late March, primarily due to softer retail sales and personal consumption data. While the New York Fed's nowcast is higher at 2.4%, it's still below the 2.5-3.0% range that most economists project for Q1. The recent Q4 2025 GDP disappointment (0.7% vs expectations) and weakening consumption trends suggest growth momentum is slowing significantly.
key_factors: Atlanta Fed GDPNow model at 1.6% as of early April (down from 2%+ in March)
Q4 2025 GDP severely disappointed at 0.7% annualized vs 3% expectations
Softer retail sales and consumer spending data weighing on forecasts
New York Fed nowcast at 2.4% still below threshold
Most economist forecasts cluster around 2.5-3.0% for Q1 but trending lower
risks: New York Fed nowcast at 2.4% suggests possibility of reaching 2.0% threshold
Final few weeks of Q1 data could surprise positively
Economic data revisions could push final reading higher
Consumer spending could rebound more than expected
Investment and government spending components could offset consumption weakness
